address: Huay Kaew Road, Suthep, Muang Chiang Mai

description.en:
## Overview

Wat Phra That Doi Suthep is the most iconic temple in Chiang Mai, perched 1,055 meters above sea level on the slopes of Doi Suthep mountain. Its glittering golden chedi is visible from across the city and has been a sacred pilgrimage site for centuries. For most visitors, no trip to Chiang Mai is complete without making the journey up the mountain to this revered shrine.

## History

The temple was founded in 1383 during the reign of King Kuena of the Lanna Kingdom. According to legend, a white elephant carrying a relic of the Buddha was set free to roam the mountainside. The elephant climbed to the top of Doi Suthep, trumpeted three times, and lay down, which was taken as a divine sign to build the temple on that exact spot. The central golden chedi is believed to enshrine a piece of the Buddha's shoulder bone, making it one of the holiest sites in northern Thailand.

## What to See

The temple complex is reached by climbing a dramatic 309-step Naga staircase, flanked by ornate serpent balustrades, or by taking the funicular railway for 20 THB. At the top, the gleaming golden chedi stands at the center of a terrace offering panoramic views over Chiang Mai city and the surrounding countryside. The complex includes beautifully detailed murals depicting the life of the Buddha, a large bronze Buddha image, and several smaller shrines. Monks chant at the base of the chedi during morning and evening prayer, creating an atmosphere of deep reverence.

## Practical Info

The temple is open daily from 6:00 AM to 6:00 PM. The entrance fee for foreign visitors is 30 THB. A modest dress code applies, so cover your shoulders and knees before entering. Red songthaew shared taxis run regularly from the base of the mountain near Chiang Mai Zoo and cost around 40 to 60 THB per person each way. The drive up takes about 20 minutes along a scenic winding road. Early morning visits offer cooler temperatures, fewer crowds, and the chance to witness monks performing their daily rituals.
description.nl: Heilige tempel op bergtop met panoramisch uitzicht over de stad en gouden chedi

best_time_to_visit: Early morning (6-8 AM) for cool weather and fewer crowds
tips: Dress modestly with shoulders and knees covered or bring a cover-up
Take a red songthaew from near Chiang Mai Zoo for about 40-60 THB each way
Climb the 309-step Naga staircase for the full experience or take the funicular for 20 THB
